content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN items in his shop (numbered 111 through NNN); for each valid iii, the price of the iii-th item is PiP_iPi​. Since Chef has very loyal customers, all NNN items are guaranteed to be sold regardless of their price.

However, the government introduced a price ceiling KKK, which means that for each item iii such that Pi>KP_i \gt KPi​>K, its price should be reduced from PiP_iPi​ to KKK.

Chef's revenue is the sum of prices of all the items he sells. Find the amount of revenue which Chef loses because of this price ceiling.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the amount of lost revenue.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: The initial revenue is 10+2+3+4+5=2410 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 = 2410+2+3+4+5=24. Because of the price ceiling, P1P_1P1​ decreases from 101010 to 444 and P5P_5P5​ decreases from 555 to 444. The revenue afterwards is 4+2+3+4+4=174 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 4 = 174+2+3+4+4=17 and the lost revenue is 24−17=724 - 17 = 724−17=7.
Test Case 2: The initial revenue is 1+2+3+4+5+6+7=281 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 + 6 + 7 = 281+2+3+4+5+6+7=28. For each valid iii, Pi≤15P_i \le 15Pi​≤15, so there are no changes, the revenue after introduction of the price ceiling is the same and there is zero lost revenue.
Test Case 3: The initial revenue is 10+9+8+7+6=4010 + 9 + 8 + 7 + 6 = 4010+9+8+7+6=40. Since Pi>5P_i \gt 5Pi​>5 for each valid iii, the prices of all items decrease to 555. The revenue afterwards is 5⋅5=255 \cdot 5 = 255⋅5=25 and the lost revenue is 40−25=1540 - 25 = 1540−25=15.
